title: Stellaris® RNA FISH Alternative Protocol for Adherent Cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9apdi","text":"This alternative adherent cell protocol utilizes a methanol based fixation which can be effective at reducing background fluorescence","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"32alf","text":"under certain circumstances.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"b7d4f","text":"Preparation of Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fqal0","text":"NOTE: When performing Stellaris RNA FISH, it is imperative to limit RNA degradation. mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d49ti","text":"900 μL Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-HB1-10)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fvc6a","text":"100 μL Deionized Formamide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5aksh","text":"NOTE: Do not freeze Hybridization Buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7obeg","text":"WARNING! Be sure to let the formamide warm to room temperature before opening the bottle.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"675lt","text":"Wash Buffer A (10 mL):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"23bpg","text":"Final composition is 10% (vol./vol.) formamide in 1X Wash Buffer A","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahrt6","text":"Mix and dilute Wash Buffer A fresh for each experiment:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ir0o","text":"For a final volume of 10 mL, mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f0qti","text":"2 mL Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer A (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-WA1-60)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fq7p8","text":"Add 7 mL Nuclease-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6vv7v","text":"Add 1 mL Deionized Formamide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dh5p9","text":"Mix well by vortexing gently.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"448ul","text":"Wash Buffer B:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fvcmc","text":"Add Nuclease-free water to Wash Buffer B bottle upon first use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e1k4j","text":"Add 88 mL of Nuclease-free water to bottle (Biosearch Technologies Cat# SMF-WB1-20) before use. Mix thoroughly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"32k24","text":"Nuclear Stain for use after hybridization:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dicv9","text":"4',6-diamidino-2-phenylindole (DAPI) prepared in Wash Buffer A (see above) at 5 ng/mL. This solution is to be used in Step J","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8aqju","text":"below.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"adf1e","text":"Mounting media:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9qudf","text":"Vectashield Mounting Medium from Vector Laboratories (#H-1000).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8u4dt","text":"NOTE: For best results, samples mounted with Vectashield Mounting Medium should be imaged the same day.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tmnh","text":"Storage Guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1rrh","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b4d9q","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes are shipped dry and can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in this state. Dissolved probe mix should be subjected","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a6tn7","text":"to a minimum number of freeze-thaw cycles. For daily and short-term use of dissolved probe mix, storage at +2 to +8 °C in the","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"65fq1","text":"dark for up to a month is recommended. For storage lasting longer than a month, we recommend aliquoting and freezing probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2c34u","text":"in the dark at -15 to -30 °C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3rvi7","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"63g13","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer should be stored at +2 to +8 °C for short-term steps: Grow cells on 18 mm round #1 coverglass in a 12-well cell culture plate.
Aspirate growth medium, and wash with 1 mL of 1X PBS. undetermined
To fix and permeabilize cells, add 1 mL of methanol-acetic acid (MeOH-AcOH) fixation solution. undetermined
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Cells can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in MeOH-AcOH up to 48 hours before hybridization. Do not use a coverglass containing adherent cells if the MeOH-AcOH has completely evaporated.
If frozen before using, warm the reconstituted probe solution to room temperature. Mix well by vortexing, then centrifuge briefly.

To prepare the Hybridization Buffer containing probe, add 1 μL of probe stock solution to 100 μL of Hybridization Buffer, and then vortex and centrifuge (enough for one coverglass). This creates a working probe solution of 125 nM. This solution will be used on steps d and e.
Aspirate the MeOH-AcOH off the coverglass containing adherent cells within the 12-well plate.
Add 1 mL of Wash Buffer A (see recipe above), and incubate at room temperature for 2-5 minutes. undetermined 00:05:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60

Assemble humidified chamber: 150 mm tissue culture plate; bottom lined evenly with a flat water-saturated paper towel and a single layer of Parafilm placed on top of the paper towel. This chamber will help prevent evaporation of the probe solution from under the coverglass.

Within the humidified chamber, dispense 100 μL of the Hybridization Buffer containing probe onto the Parafilm. undetermined Stellaris(R) RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-HB1-10
Gently transfer the coverglass, cells side down, onto the 100 μL drop of Hybridization Buffer containing probe.
Cover the humidified chamber with the tissue culture lid, and seal with Parafilm.
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 2 hours. (Incubation can be continued up to 16 hours). 02:00:00
Gently transfer the coverglass, cells side up, to a fresh 12-well plate containing 1 mL of Wash Buffer A. undetermined Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00

Aspirate Wash Buffer A, and then add 1 mL of DAPI nuclear stain (Wash Buffer A consisting of 5 ng/mL DAPI) to counterstain the nuclei. undetermined Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes. 00:30:00

Aspirate the DAPI staining buffer, and then add 1 mL of Wash Buffer B. Incubate at room temperature for 2-5 minutes. undetermined 00:05:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer B Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WB1-20

Add a small drop (approximately 15 μL) of Vectashield Mounting Medium onto a microscope slide, and mount coverglass onto the slide, cells side down. undetermined VECTASHIELD Mounting Medium Vector Laboratories Catalog #H-1000
Gently wick away excess anti-fade from the perimeter of the coverglass.
Seal the coverglass perimeter with clear nail polish, and allow to dry.
If necessary, gently wipe away any dried salt off the coverglass using water.
Proceed to Imaging
